name: Nebraska Medical Nutrition Therapist License
description: Already licensed as a medical nutrition therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Nebraska - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Medical Nutrition Therapy Licensure
provider.parentOrganization: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services (DHHS)

address.description: DHHS, Credentialing Division, P.O. Box 94986, Lincoln, NE 68509-4986

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by Sept 1 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Verification of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Age & Legal Presence
requiredDocuments.description: A copy of a birth certificate, driver's license, passport, or similar documentation.

requiredDocuments.name: Fingerprint Background check
requiredDocuments.description: Required for all new applications starting in 2026.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Nebraska LMNT license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is $114.

mainEntity.name: When does the Nebraska license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Nebraska LMNT licenses expire on September 1 of all odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $114.

mainEntity.name: Is the title 'Dietitian' licensed in Nebraska?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Nebraska licenses the specific practice of Medical Nutrition Therapy, granting the title Licensed Medical Nutrition Therapist (LMNT). General nutrition services may not require a license, but only an LMNT can provide medical nutrition therapy.

mainEntity.name: Does Nebraska have reciprocity for medical nutrition therapists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Nebraska has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, medical nutrition therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my medical nutrition therapist license to Nebraska?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Nebraska while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
